analysis: The market sentiment is decidedly negative, though not in panic mode. The page is dominated by news of the Iran War entering its fifth week, with oil prices surging above $100 per barrel amid threats to energy infrastructure. Major indexes are holding slightly positive territory (+0.22% to +0.52%) but the dominant narrative is one of geopolitical uncertainty, oil shock concerns, and tech sector weakness. VIX remains elevated at 29.95 despite declining 3.54%. Gold is up 0.67% showing safe haven flows. Headlines focus on Trump threatening Iran energy sites, Red Sea conflict concerns, and 'stunning plunges' in Magnificent 7 tech stocks. Rating of 4/10 reflects below-neutral sentiment due to war escalation and elevated fear indicators, but avoids extreme negativity since markets show resilience and aren't collapsing.
